title: AUTISM SPECTRUM DISORDER PROGRAM - 211info
text: NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT AUTISM SPECTRUM DISORDER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides licensed special education teachers with specific teaching experience, training, and knowledge in the area of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). These services support districts as they work to build the capacity of their special education teams. Examples of this work include developing staff knowledge of the characteristics of ASD, classroom-based strategies, evidence-based practices (EBPs), and assessment requirements and procedures. 5825 NE Ray Circle Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Ages 5-21, medical or health assessment is required as part of the educational eligibility process Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 614-1310 - - .12.. -.12...12.. Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (888) 990-7500 Toll-free Main phone (503) 614-1310 Tina Meier-Nowell tina Meier-nowell [email protected] . Tina Meier-Nowell [email protected] [email protected] llamar Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Columbia, Tillamook, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus line 48 and Orenco Station MAX stop. If needed, transportation is arranged with the child's resident school district. Services: Pediatric Developmental Assessment * Autism Spectrum Disorder Student Disability Services * Autism Spectrum Disorder Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - ENGLISH LANGUAGE LEARNER AND MIGRANT EDUCATION SERVICES EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - HOME SCHOOL REGISTRATION REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - EARLY INTERVENTION AND EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
